div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes — chain is running

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no — idle

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
independent path:
(is_running was never set —
no debounce or re-arm needed)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
sync-group path:
(is_running is true —
must debounce, then clear it)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(207, 230, 240) !important; text-align: center; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; width: 200px;
text-align: left;

span.div:
Key concepts — read this first
• Space — a macOS virtual desktop on a single display. Each display has its own list.
• Sync group — a list of displays that should track each other. When any one switches Space, the others follow.
• Trigger display — the display the user actually switched. Target display — another display in the same sync group that we drive to match.
• Watcher = hs.spaces.watcher. Fires our callback on any Space change. Does NOT tell us which display — we have to diff.
• gotoSpace = hs.spaces.gotoSpace. Programmatically switches a display to a given Space. macOS drops rapid back-to-back calls — hence switchDelay.
• lastActiveSpaces — snapshot of prior per-display Space state. We diff against this to find what changed.
• is_running (roadmap rename of syncInProgress) — simple boolean. true while dispatching gotoSpace calls and through the post-chain debounce. Single global flag, not per-group.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
User switches a Space.
Via Mission Control gesture, ⌃→/⌃←,
a Dock click, or other macOS UI.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
macOS commits the switch.
The chosen Space is now active on that display.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Watcher fires our callback.
hs.spaces.watcher invokes our Lua callback —
the only signal we get from macOS that something changed.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is SpacesSync enabled?
(false after the user toggles off via the
toggle hotkey or :stop)

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Return — SpacesSync is off.
How we got here: user pressed the toggle hotkey
(default ⌃⌥⌘Y), or called spoon.SpacesSync:stop()
from init.lua, the Hammerspoon console, or hs -c.
Why we still need this guard: when :stop runs,
macOS may already be partway through calling our
watcher function. You can't kill an in-flight call —
so this guard makes that one straggler bail out
instead of finishing a sync the user just stopped.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Already running a sync chain?
is_running == true while we are dispatching
gotoSpace calls AND through the post-chain
debounce window.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Drop the event.
This is the whole point of is_running:
swallow our own gotoSpace echoes and the
phantom fires that follow Space animations.
Side effect: real user swipes inside this
~1.4s window are also dropped (see design panel).

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Diff to find what changed.
Compare hs.spaces.activeSpaces against lastActiveSpaces
to find which display(s) just switched.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Did anything actually change?
Did at least one display's active Space change
since the last snapshot?

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
No actual Space change — exit.
The watcher fires on more than real Space switches:
Mission Control opening, display reconfiguration,
animation settling, Stage Manager events.
The diff confirms nothing actually changed —
refresh the snapshot defensively and exit.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Find the trigger's sync group.
Map the trigger display's UUID → position number,
then scan syncGroups for a group containing that position.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Trigger in a sync group?
Is the trigger display a member of any group?

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Independent display — nothing to sync.
Trigger is not in any sync group.
We never set is_running on this path,
so there's no chain to wait out and
no flag to clear afterward.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Set is_running = true.
From now until the post-chain debounce expires,
watcher fires are dropped.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Iterate target displays.
Targets = sync-group members minus the trigger.
Start at i = 1.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Sync this target to the trigger's index.
1. Compute the Space ID at the trigger's index
on this target display.
2. Dispatch hs.spaces.gotoSpace targetSpaceID.
3. macOS animates the Space switch on the target.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
More targets?
Any target displays remaining in the sync group?

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Show popup on trigger display.
The active Space index with names from the
workspace name store. Auto-dismisses after popupDuration.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Start debounce timer (0.8 s).
is_running stays true through this window so
phantom fires from animation settling are still
dropped (they fire after the last gotoSpace dispatch).

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Update lastActiveSpaces baseline.
Set lastActiveSpaces = currentSpaces
so the next watcher fire diffs against
the post-switch state.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Watcher armed.
Ready for the next user Space switch.

div.style: color: rgb(240, 232, 200) !important; text-align: center; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; width: 200px;
text-align: left;

span.div:
Design intent — what we accept and what we don't try to fix
The goal: only human-initiated swipes trigger the sync system. Our own gotoSpace calls must NOT cause secondary syncs.
How we achieve it: a single global is_running boolean. Set true at chain start, cleared after debounce. Every watcher fire during that window is dropped, no questions asked.
What gets dropped (intentionally):
• ✅ Echoes of our own gotoSpace dispatches — the whole point
• ✅ Phantom fires from macOS Space animations settling — happens during debounce, also dropped
What gets dropped (accepted side effect):
• ⚠ Real user swipes inside the ~1.4s ignore window. We accept this. Users don't typically swipe multiple times in under a second; if it happens, the user just swipes again. Not worth solving.
What we deliberately don't build:
• ⊘ Pending trigger / drain step / bounded retry loop — overengineering for a use case that doesn't exist.
• ⊘ Echo classifier (compare changed Space against expectedActiveSpaces) — solves the same non-problem.
• ⊘ Eager baseline update (write lastActiveSpaces[target] before each gotoSpace) — would let us drop is_running entirely, but adds correctness risk if gotoSpace is silently dropped by macOS. Not worth the invasiveness.
Difference from CURRENT: functionally none. Possible cosmetic improvement: rename syncInProgress to is_running for readability. That's it.

svg.text: SpacesSync — runtime sync workflow (ROADMAP — same shape as current; documents design intent)
